preamble: The shift from the voiceless palatal plosive [c] to the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] is a classic example of palatalization followed by spirantization. This transition is famously documented in the evolution from Latin to French, where [k] before [a] passed through a palatal stage [c] before becoming [ʃ].

examples.note: Latin [k] before [a] palatalized to [c] in early Gallo-Romance before spirantizing.
Regular palatalization and spirantization of initial velars before [a].

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
